title: linaria glauca subsp. aragonensis
intro: Linaria glauca subsp. aragonensis, also known as the Aragon blue-purplespike, is a subspecies of the Linaria glauca plant, a type of flowering plant in the figwort family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and is believed to have medicinal properties that can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Linaria glauca subsp. aragonensis include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
